zh.Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal: 葡萄牙布拉加布拉加庫尼亞
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães於1240年出生在葡萄牙瓜亞達時，她的父親Ruy Martins de Nomães25歲，母親Beatriz Anes Redondo20歲。她於大約1260年與Pedro Paes De Alvarenga在葡萄牙結婚。她去世於1326年在葡萄牙可布拉享年86歲，埋葬在葡萄牙可布拉。

zh-hans.Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal: 葡萄牙布拉加布拉加庫尼亞
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães于1240年出生在葡萄牙瓜亞達时，她的父亲Ruy Martins de Nomães25岁，母亲Beatriz Anes Redondo20岁。她于大约1260年与Pedro Paes De Alvarenga在葡萄牙结婚。她去世于1326年在葡萄牙可布拉享年86岁，埋葬在葡萄牙可布拉。

nameMeanings.name: Rodrigues
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Luis, Mario, Fernando, Alberto, Juan, Julio, Armando, Eduardo, Francisco.  Portuguese Joao, Agostinho, Paulo, Joaquim, Guilherme, Ilidio, Serafim, Vasco, Albano, Albeiro, Amadeu, Anabela.

Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the personal name   Rodrigo  . This surname is also found in the cities of the west coast of India, having been taken there by Portuguese colonists. Compare   Rodrigue   2.
Spanish: variant or altered form of Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodriques
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Fernando, Francisco, Gilberto, Juan, Adriano, Agusto, Alfredo, Alicia.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo.
Altered form of Portuguese Rodrigues and probably also of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ). It is common in Brazil.

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

nameMeanings.name: Ruiz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Pedro, Ramon, Jorge.
Spanish and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Ruy, a short form of Rodrigo .

variants.name: Ruisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Santo, Angelo, Matteo, Carmela, Lorenzo, Pasquale, Vittorio.
Italian (Sicily): probably an Italianized form of Spanish   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Ruz
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

variants.name: Ruis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Arturo, Carlos, Conception, Elba, Fernando, Jose, Josue, Juan, Jubenal, Lazaro, Lupe.
Spanish and Portuguese: variant of   Ruiz  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Joana
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães was born in 1240, in Guarda, Portugal, her father, Ruy Martins de Nomães, was 25 and her mother, Beatriz Anes Redondo, was 20. She married Pedro Paes De Alvarenga about 1260, in Portugal. She died in 1326, in Coimbra, Portugal, at the age of 86, and was buried in Coimbra, Portugal.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães est née en 1240, à Guarda, Portugal, son père, Ruy Martins de Nomães, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Beatriz Anes Redondo, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Pedro Paes De Alvarenga environ 1260, à Portugal. Elle est décédée en 1326, à Coimbra, Portugal, à l’âge de 86 ans, et a été enterrée à Coimbra, Portugal.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães 1240 in Guarda, Portugal geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ruy Martins de Nomães 25 und ihre Mutter Beatriz Anes Redondo 20. Sie hat um 1260 in Portugal Pedro Paes De Alvarenga geheiratet. Sie starb 1326 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Coimbra, Portugal und wurde in Coimbra, Portugal begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães è nata il nel 1240 nel Guarda, Portogallo, sua padre, Ruy Martins de Nomães, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Beatriz Anes Redondo, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Pedro Paes De Alvarenga circa 1260, nel Portogallo. Morì nel 1326, nel Coimbra, Portogallo, all'età di 86 anni, e fu sepolta a Coimbra, Portogallo.

ja.lifeSummary: Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomãesが1240年にポルトガルグアルダで生まれた時，父Ruy Martins de Nomãesは25歳，母Beatriz Anes Redondoは20歳であった。約1260年にポルトガルでPedro Paes De Alvarengaと結婚した。彼女は1326年にポルトガルコインブラで86歳で死去し，ポルトガルコインブラにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães(이)가 1240년 포르투갈 구아르다에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ruy Martins de Nomães(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatriz Anes Redondo(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1260년 포르투갈에서 Pedro Paes De Alvarenga(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1326년 포르투갈 코임브라에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 포르투갈 코임브라에 묻혔다.

historicEvents.title: Portugal
historicEvents.description: Portugal is one of the oldest nations in Europe having established its continental frontier as it is set today in 1297

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães nasceu em 1240, em Guarda, Portugal, seu pai, Ruy Martins de Nomães, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Beatriz Anes Redondo, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Pedro Paes De Alvarenga aproximadamente 1260, em Portugal. Ela faleceu em 1326, em Coimbra, Portugal, com 86 anos, e foi sepultada em Coimbra, Portugal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães родилась в 1240 г. в местности гуарда, Португалия, её отцу по имени Ruy Martins de Nomães было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatriz Anes Redondo, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Pedro Paes De Alvarenga около 1260 г. в местности Португалия. Она умерла в 1326 г. в местности коимбра, Португалия в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности коимбра, Португалия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães nació en 1240, en Guarda, Portugal, su papá, Ruy Martins de Nomães, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Beatriz Anes Redondo, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Pedro Paes De Alvarenga aproximadamente 1260, en Portugal. Murió en 1326, en Coimbra, Portugal, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultada en Coimbra, Portugal.
